text.title: semantic-xml-content Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for semantic-xml-content as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Carries no role information. Agent gains nothing over Markdown.

failure-mode.description: Mixing `<rule>` in one file and `<policy>` or `<requirement>` in another for the same concept.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Agents can apply policy by tag only when the vocabulary is closed and consistent.

failure-mode.description: Wrapping every paragraph in `<text>`, `<content>`, `<block>`, or `<item>` when no role distinction exists.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Adds depth without information; hurts both human readers and agent token economy.

failure-mode.description: Embedding angle brackets or ampersands in element text without CDATA or entity escaping.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Parser breaks. Agent sees corrupted content or refuses to load the file.

failure-mode.description:
One giant `

` containing a rule, a rationale, an example, and three see-also links.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Defeats the point of XML. Split into typed siblings: `<rule>`, `<example>`, `<see-also>`.

failure-mode.description: Indenting for aesthetics, adding empty lines between every tag, embedding ASCII-art borders.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Agent does not care about layout. Whitespace is noise; ASCII art is forbidden by repo rules anyway.
